items.headline: City Council reviews $2027 capital spending plans
items.summary: The council held a study session on the Fiscal Year 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program, which outlines major infrastructure and facility projects the city plans to fund. This is worth monitoring if you care about what gets built or improved in Mountain View over the next year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: City Council will introduce an ordinance amending the Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: City releases Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan
items.summary: The City Council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, which outlines improvements to San José's library system. This plan could affect which library branches get renovated, expanded, or enhanced services that residents use.

items.headline: Youth services programs report on annual outcomes
items.summary: City Council heard annual reports from multiple youth programs including the San José Youth Empowerment Alliance and Safe Summer Initiative on April 9. These reports show how city-funded youth services are performing and what impact they're having on local young people.

items.headline: Downtown historic building eligible for density bonus
items.summary: City Council is considering elevating the historic status of 405 Kipling Street, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This affects what can be built on a downtown property and how historic preservation is valued in development.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is proposing to rezone and redevelop two properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and about 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant new housing and a major land use change for that corridor.

items.headline: Development project at 333 Franklin Street hearing postponed
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled and rescheduled for April 22, 2026. Residents who were notified about the April 8 hearing should watch for a new notice about when the project will actually be reviewed.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents should monitor for details about what construction project this contract covers.
